Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 341 [Birdcall Text][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-ri-ba-nu-⸢u₂mušen⸣ [MUŠEN] ⸢d⸣AMAR.UTU IGI.LA₂-šu₂ NIG₂.TUK UR.SAG!(SUR₂) GAL BE BE.BE ša₂-niš-ma ba-aʾ-i-ru <GU₃.GU₃si>

The arabānû is [the bird] of Marduk. Its appearance is luxuriant. It continually calls: "Great hero, lord of lords," or "Hunter".

o 22

DAR.LUGAL⸢mušen⸣ MUŠEN d⸢EN⸣.[ME.ŠAR₂].RA ⸢taḫ⸣-ta-ṭa a-na dtu-⸢tu⸣ GU₃.GU₃si

The rooster is the bird of Enmešara. It continually calls: "You have done wrong towards Tutu."

o 33

it-ti-du-u₂mušen ⸢MUŠEN⸣ [d]PAP.SUKKAL ⸢d⸣ka₃-ka₃ uḫ-ta-bil a-na gišTUKUL RI ŠID.ŠID

The francolin is the bird of Papsukkal. It continually recites: "Kakka has done wrong to the weapon ...."

o 44

I₅.GARmušen ⸢MUŠEN⸣ dx x ⸢qu₃⸣-du-du ap-pa-šu₂ TE-MEŠ-šu₂ tak₃-⸢ka dDUMU.ZI⸣ lu₂SIPA KI.MIN<(ŠID.ŠID)>

The heron is the bird of the god .... Its beak is bowed, its cheeks are depressed. Ditto (it continually recites): "Dumuzi, the shepherd."

o 55

si-im!(IḪ-)kurmušen MUŠEN ⸢dnar⸣-ru-⸢du⸣ u₈-u-a u₈-u-a ⸢GU₃.GU₃⸣si

The singurgurtu is the bird of Narudu. It continually calls: "Woe! Woe!"

o 66

šu-u₂-šu-rumušen MUŠEN AN.ŠAR₂ ki-ki-iʾ muš-šur-u GU₃.⸢GU₃⸣si

The suššuru is the bird of Anšar. It continually calls: "How are they left free?"

o 77

zi-i-bumušen ⸢MUŠEN d⸣x [...] x x [...] UR.SAG dIM.DUGUD in-e-ri KI.MIN<(GU₃.GU₃si)>

The vulture is the bird of the god ... [...]. It continually calls: "... [...], the hero, killed Anzu."

o 88

raq-raq-qimušen ⸢MUŠEN⸣ DUMU ⸢LUGAL⸣ [...] x [...] ri-i-qa ri-i-qa-a la-pa-an UR.SAG dMAŠ KI.MIN<(GU₃.GU₃si)>

The stork is the bird of the crown prince. It continually calls: "[...] ... [...]. Go away! Go away from the hero Ninurta!"

o 99

šu-nu-nu-tu₂mušen ⸢MUŠEN⸣ IB [...] ⸢dba⸣-[ba₆] ⸢KI⸣.MIN MUŠEN tam-ti₃ šu-nir-zu ušumgal ka-bi-ta

The swallow is the bird of ... [...] Baba, alternatively: the bird of Tiamat. "šu-nir-zu ušumgal ka-bi-ta

o 1010

<uš₂> nu e₃ ⸢KI.MIN⸣ uš₁₁ ⸢nu⸣ [bi]-⸢iz⸣ :. ⸢bi-iz⸣-bi-iz-a šum-gal-lu₄!(BU) ul-tu₂ KA-šu₂ UŠ₂ <la₃> E₃a KI.MIN

uš₂ nu e₃," alternatively "uš₁₁ nu bi-iz : bi-iz-bi-iz-a," (translated:) "(Your emblem) is the great dragon from whose mouth no blood comes out," alternatively,

o 1111

⸢im⸣-tu la in-⸢na-tuk⸣-ku GU₃.GU₃si

"no poison trickles," it continually calls.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[ku]-ub-sa-anmušen MUŠEN d⸢NUSKA⸣ d⸢SIRSIR d⸣AMAR.UTU lu₂MA₂.LAḪ₄ tam-ti₃ dMUATI ša₂-qu-u dMUATI dan-nu ša₂ ZA KIN/KAB ŠU₂

The kubšānu is the bird of Nusku. Sirsir is Marduk, the sailor of the sea. Sublime Nabu, mighty Nabu, who ....

r 22

ka-ti-matmušen ⸢MUŠEN⸣ a-⸢ḫat⸣ d7 ŠEŠ ⸢AB?⸣ KA GA da-nu₃ ⸢GU₃⸣.GU₃si

The katimattu is the bird of the sister of the Seven. It continually calls: "Brother, ... of Anu."

r 33

kur-ki-imušen ⸢MUŠEN⸣ d⸢A₂⸣.SAG₃ la ka-bar-tim-ma <ul?> E₁₁ ina!(U) qa-qa-ri me-e ina NAGe-[šu₂]  ; UŠ₂ u₂-ba-u₂-ni ⸢GU₃⸣.[GU₃si]

The goose is the bird of Asakku. It cannot go up from the ground without (its) kabbartu.1 When it is drinking water, it continually calls: "They sought to kill me."

r 44

aḫ-ru-⸢ḫa-ru?mušen MUŠEN d⸣BAD [...] INIM DUMU NIBRUki INIM.GAR SIG₅ KI.MIN<(GU₃.GU₃si)>

The raven is the bird of Ellil. It continually calls: "[...] ... the word of Nippur's citizen [...] a good reputation."

r 55

SUR₂.DU₃mušen MUŠEN dAMAR.UTU d⸢ŠA₃⸣.ZU mu-du-u lib₃-bi DINGIR ša!(ŠU₂) i-ba-ru kar-ši KI.MIN<(GU₃.GU₃si)>

The hawk is the bird of Marduk. It continually calls: "Šazu, who knows the gods' hearts, who sees into (men's) minds."

r 66

BURU₅.ḪABRUD.DA <MUŠEN> dA₂.SAG₃ ṣi-i dA₂.SAG₃ ṣi-i dA₂.SAG₃ GU₃.GU₃si

The rock-partridge is the bird of Asakku. It continually calls: "Shoo, Asakku! Shoo, Asakku!"

1 Could this refer to the fact that geese cannot fly for 3 or 4 weeks during moulting , when they are shedding their old feathers and growing new ones?
